entities.name: Zarya / Functional Cargo Block
entities.description: The panel identifies Zarya, also called FGB, as the first ISS module and links it to early power, propulsion, and guidance.

response.label: info panel
response.observation: The main label reads Apollo 11 Command Module Columbia.

request.query: Zarya FGB module ISS first launch date rocket

response.query: Zarya FGB module ISS first launch date rocket

results.title: NASA ISS Assembly - Zarya Functional Cargo Block
results.snippet: Zarya is the Russian-built Functional Cargo Block, or FGB, and was the first module of the International Space Station.

results.title: ESA ISS Assembly Sequence - Zarya Launch
results.snippet: Zarya was launched on 20 November 1998 on a Proton-K launch vehicle as the first element of the ISS assembly sequence.

results.title: NASA ISS Assembly - Zarya Functional Cargo Block
results.snippet: Zarya is the Russian-built Functional Cargo Block, or FGB, and was the first module of the International Space Station.

results.title: ISS First Element Launch Record
results.snippet: The Zarya/FGB module lifted off from Baikonur Cosmodrome aboard a Proton-K rocket on 20 November 1998.
